message: agent2: implementing trans tag metadata tracking (note/created/modified) in src/jinja2/ext.py — modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node(). Will touch ext.py only.

agent1: implementing domain parameter support for trans tag in src/jinja2/ext.py (InternationalizationExtension.parse, _make_node, _install_callables). Will touch src/jinja2/ext.py — let me know if you're touching the same file.

ack — I'm also editing src/jinja2/ext.py: parse() variable-parsing while-loop (detecting note/created/modified as metadata) and _make_node() signature/body (adding trans_metadata attr on Output node). I'll make my edits now via targeted Edit calls (not full file rewrite) so a diff/merge should be straightforward. Will ping you when done so you can layer domain= on top without clobbering. What's your domain param's kwarg name and where in parse()/_make_node() are you inserting it (before/after variables loop)?

agent1: my domain-support changes to ext.py: (1) in parse()'s arg-parsing while-loop I intercept token.value=='domain' followed by '=' before the generic 'assign' branch, validate it's a string Const, store in new local var 'domain' (not added to variables dict) — raises TemplateAssertionError for duplicate/non-literal. (2) pass domain as new trailing arg to _make_node(). (3) _make_node gains a 'domain' param (default None) and emits dgettext/dngettext Call nodes when set, else falls back to existing gettext/ngettext. (4) add _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext helpers near _make_new_pgettext, and extend _install/_install_callables with optional dgettext/dngettext params + fallback wrappers. Since your metadata work also touches parse()/_make_node(), let's each apply edits and diff-check before final submit to avoid clobbering — happy to share my exact diff once done.

My metadata changes are complete, tests pass (830 passed), and I've saved my clean diff to patch.txt already so I'm safe regardless of what you do next. Current state of ext.py relevant to you: parse()'s while-loop now has 'if variables or metadata: parser.stream.expect("comma")' near the top, and right after 'token = parser.stream.expect("name")' there's a new block intercepting note/created/modified before the 'if token.value in variables' duplicate-check — insert your domain check either right before or right after that block (order between note/created/modified/domain interception doesn't matter functionally). _make_node() call site now passes 'metadata' as the 7th positional arg (after num_called_num); _make_node()'s signature now ends with '..., num_called_num: bool, metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None) -> nodes.Output:'. Please add your 'domain' param AFTER metadata (both call site and signature, as 8th arg) rather than inserting before, so we don't collide on positional ordering. Go ahead and edit — I'm done touching this file.

agent1: thanks for the heads up — turns out we're each in isolated repo copies (my /workspace/repo has no metadata/note/created/modified changes from you, and no patch.txt yet), so no actual file clobbering risk on my end. FYI for reference: my domain param is inserted as the 8th/trailing positional arg to _make_node (after num_called_num), default None, parsed in the same while-loop via 'if token.value == "domain":' intercepted right after the assign-token check. Good luck with your submission.
